---
title: "Firing Test Report"
author: "PEGASUS DACS"
format: pdf
jupyter: pegasus-venv
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
params:
  mission_id: "PEG_ENG1_INJ1_Firing3"
---

```{python}
#| include: false

import sys
print(sys.executable)
print(sys.path)


import os
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

from src.db import connect, get_series_by_subsystem, get_console_messages, get_massflows #, get_metadata
from src.plotting_functions import plot_by_sensor, plot_total_massflow, plot_equivalence_ratio

import sys
print(sys.executable)
print(sys.path)


# .env datei laden mit richtigen parametern für connection
load_dotenv()

URL    = os.environ["INFLUX_URL"]
TOKEN  = os.environ["INFLUX_TOKEN"]
ORG    = os.environ["INFLUX_ORG"]
BUCKET = os.environ["INFLUX_BUCKET"]

client = connect(URL, TOKEN, ORG)

# Hole den Parameter aus dem YAML ganz oben
try:
    mission_id = params["mission_id"]
except NameError:
    mission_id = "PEG_ENG1_INJ1_Firing3"  # Fallback für direktes Ausführen

if not mission_id:
    raise RuntimeError("Pass mission_id via: quarto render report.qmd -P mission_id:FIRING_...")

# comment: meta, START, END = get_metadata(client, BUCKET, mission_id)



# Alle Subsystems die geplotted werden sollen
SUBSYSTEMS = ["FSS", "OSS", "IGN", "ENG", "INJ", "VAR"]


# (<Kategoriename>, (<Name measurement in DB>, <Y-Achsenbeschriftung>))
CATEGORIES = [
    ("Massflow", ("massflowSensor", "Massflow [g/s]")),   # measurement, field
    ("Loadcell", ("loadcell", "Weight [KG]")),  # optional: weight
    ("Pressure Sensor", ("pressureSensor", "Pressure [bar]")),  # optional: dynamic_pressure
    ("Thermocouple", ("temperatureSensor", "Temperature [°C]")),
]
```

```{python}
#| echo: false

# Get console messages from db 
df_console_messages = get_console_messages(client, BUCKET, mission_id)

# Extract only entries that indicate sequences
seq_df = df_console_messages[df_console_messages["value"].str.startswith("[SEQ]")].copy()

# Get date of test and display it along with mission id
test_date = seq_df["time"].iloc[0].strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
mission_id_tex = mission_id.replace("_", r"\_")
display(
    Markdown(rf"\begin{{center}}\textbf{{Mission ID:}} {mission_id_tex}, \textbf{{Test Date:}} {test_date}\end{{center}}"))

# Manually configurable boundaries for plotting based on console messages
START_MSG = "[SEQ] Firing Sequence initiated"
END_MSG = "[SEQ] Firing Sequence complete"

# Keep rows from first start match onward
if START_MSG:
    start_idx = seq_df.index[seq_df["value"].eq(START_MSG)]
    if len(start_idx) > 0:
        seq_df = seq_df.loc[start_idx[0]:]

# Keep rows up to and including first end match
if END_MSG:
    end_idx = seq_df.index[seq_df["value"].eq(END_MSG)]
    if len(end_idx) > 0:
        seq_df = seq_df.loc[:end_idx[0]]

# Create combined_seq_times df containing start, end, buffered start, and buffered end times for plotting
relative_buffer_time = 0.1
combined_seq_times = []

start = seq_df.iloc[0]["time"]
end = seq_df.iloc[-1]["time"]
buffer = relative_buffer_time * (end - start)
start_buf = start - buffer
end_buf = end + buffer
combined_seq_times.append((start, end, start_buf, end_buf))

# Get other events within firing sequnces
events_in_window = seq_df[(seq_df["time"] >= start) & (seq_df["time"] <= end)]

# Get ignition and shutdown times
ignition_rows = seq_df.loc[seq_df["value"].eq("[SEQ] Ignition"), "time"]
shutdown_rows = seq_df.loc[seq_df["value"].eq("[SEQ] Initiate Shutdown"), "time"]

if len(ignition_rows) != 1:
    raise RuntimeError(f"Expected exactly 1 '[SEQ] Ignition', found {len(ignition_rows)}")

if len(shutdown_rows) != 1:
    raise RuntimeError(f"Expected exactly 1 '[SEQ] Initiate Shutdown', found {len(shutdown_rows)}")

ignition_time = ignition_rows.iloc[0]
shutdown_time = shutdown_rows.iloc[0]

buffer_ignition = relative_buffer_time * (shutdown_time - ignition_time)
start_buf_ignition = ignition_time - buffer_ignition
end_buf_ignition = shutdown_time + buffer_ignition

```


```{python}
#| echo: false

# Plot only the time interval of buffered sequence and indicate the sequence time interval
for start, end, start_buf, end_buf in combined_seq_times:
    for i, subsystem in enumerate(SUBSYSTEMS):
        # Prüfe ob dieses Subsystem irgendwelche Daten hat
        has_data = False
        subsystem_data = {}
        for cat_name, (measurement, ylabel) in CATEGORIES:
            df = get_series_by_subsystem(client, BUCKET, measurement, mission_id, subsystem)
            subsystem_data[cat_name] = (df, ylabel)
            if not df.empty:
                has_data = True
        
        if not has_data:
            continue  # Subsystem komplett überspringen
        
        # für jedes Subsystem eine neue Seite anfangen
        if i > 0:
            display(Markdown(r"\newpage"))
        display(Markdown(f"# Subsystem: {subsystem}"))
        for cat_name, (df, ylabel) in subsystem_data.items():
            if df.empty:
                continue
            display(Markdown(f"## {cat_name}"))
            plot_by_sensor(
                df,
                f"{subsystem} – {cat_name}",
                ylabel,
                time_range=(start_buf, end_buf),
                highlight_range=(start, end),
                events=events_in_window,
                x0_time=start
            )
```

```{python}
#| echo: false

# Plot total massflow
df_mf = get_massflows(client, BUCKET, mission_id)

plot_total_massflow(df_mf, 
                time_range=(start_buf, end_buf),
                highlight_range=(start, end),
                events=events_in_window,
                x0_time=start)

# Plot equivalence ration between ignition and shutdown
plot_equivalence_ratio(df_mf, 
                time_range=(start_buf_ignition, end_buf_ignition),
                highlight_range=(ignition_time, shutdown_time),                
                x0_time=ignition_time)

```
